label: true-dup
a: User account deletion uses soft-delete: set deleted_at timestamp, scrub PII fields (email, name, address) immediately for GDPR compliance. Keep the record shell for referential integrity.
b: GDPR deletion implementation: mark user as deleted (deleted_at = now()), overwrite personal data fields with placeholders immediately, but preserve the row. Hard deletion would break foreign key constraints on orders, reviews, and activity logs.
reviewer_notes: Both describe the same soft-delete with immediate PII scrub for GDPR while keeping the row for FK integrity.
